authoritative_sources.title: Selling on the Internet: Prompt Delivery Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection authority for unfair or deceptive commerce practices. This guidance is authoritative for restaurant order promises, delays, cancellations, and prompt refunds when reported customer outcomes depend on those records.
used_for: guest-impact caveats for pickup and delivery reporting

authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ's Title III ADA regulations are the authoritative federal public-accommodation baseline for restaurants and hospitality venues, making them authoritative when reservation, queue, or guest-access reporting intersects accessibility obligations.
used_for: guest-service caveats when accessibility posture affects the output

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Handbook 130 - Current Edition
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST Office of Weights and Measures publishes the public baseline used by many U.S. jurisdictions for price verification and price-accuracy procedures, making it authoritative for menu-price versus POS-price evidence handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Nutrition Labeling Information for Restaurants & Retail Establishments
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA is the primary federal authority for menu-labeling requirements in covered restaurant and retail-food contexts. This guidance is authoritative for guest-facing disclosure limits on menu and nutrition claims inside reporting outputs.

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Food Code
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's Food Code is the public foodservice and retail-food safety baseline used by many jurisdictions. It is authoritative for restaurant workflow terminology and for preventing unsupported guest-safety conclusions in reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customer exception handling with policy-aware escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is the authoritative public baseline for recognizing identity-theft red flags and deciding when loyalty, booking, account, or tender anomalies must route to fraud or loss-prevention owners.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customer exception handling with policy-aware escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Protecting Personal Information: A Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is an authoritative public reference for protecting customer information and handling sensitive guest, reservation, and payment records safely during reporting workflows.
used_for: limits on exposing payment or personal data in reports

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized cross-brand standards body for payment-card security. PCI DSS is authoritative for merchants that store, process, or transmit cardholder data, which constrains payment-record handling and related reporting evidence usage.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Escalation for stale, conflicting, or incomplete data

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC KPI guidance is the authoritative public baseline for how operational metrics must be defined, contextualized, caveated, and updated when restaurant reporting is used in executive, lender, or investor-style disclosure.

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect POS, PMS, booking, CRM, service-desk, refund, payment, and inventory records and determine whether the evidence set is current and sufficient for the reporting request.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Metric calculation or report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Resolve metric logic, denominators, period cutoffs, same-store rules, and source precedence before narrative reporting or publication-ready package assembly begins.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis or disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Assemble restaurant report language, caveats, thresholds, and confidence notes while ensuring guest-facing, executive, or disclosure outputs do not imply unsupported certainty, causality, or signoff.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Escalation for stale, conflicting, or incomplete data
workflow_stages.description: Preserve unresolved source conflicts, stale feeds, missing close processes, and unsupported claims rather than publishing through them.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: High-volume intake or service execution
workflow_stages.description: Keep reporting grounded in dinner rushes, holiday weekends, delivery surges, and large catering events without turning the lane into frontline command authority.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Customer exception handling with policy-aware escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route refund exceptions, pricing overrides, guest-harm indicators, fraud-sensitive patterns, and safety-sensitive incidents to the correct adjacent specialist with complete evidence packaging.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: orders, checks, reservations, waitlist states, and guest or customer records
POS transactions, payment records, refund records, chargebacks, comps, voids, and service-recovery credits
promotion calendars, price tables, menu versions, same-store lists, and item availability states
inventory snapshots, waste and spoilage logs, inventory adjustments, and close-period reconciliation notes
PMS records, booking-system extracts, CRM notes, guest complaints, and service-desk tickets
allergen or food-safety incident notes, suspicious-tender records, and loss-prevention artifacts
metric dictionaries, reporting calendars, disclosure templates, signoff checklists, and approval matrices

allowed: Classify restaurant reporting requests and verify the minimum prerequisite records, definitions, cadence, and audience context before recommendations.
Produce evidence-linked metric definitions, reporting packages, reconciliation notes, caveat packages, and submission summaries without final commercial or safety judgment.
Escalate to adjacent owners when refund exceptions, pricing authority, fraud, guest harm, food safety, or disclosure signoff determine the answer.
Refuse unsupported trend, benchmark, causal, policy, or disclosure claims when data quality or source hierarchy is insufficient.
escalate: Missing order, payment, refund, reservation, guest, or inventory records materially change the interpretation.
Conflicts between POS, PMS, booking, pricing, finance, or service records create unreconciled metric outcomes.
Customer harm, allergen or food-safety posture, refund exception, pricing authority, or fraud sensitivity changes whether the requested output is safe.
Tenant policy, jurisdictional rule, reporting cadence, or approval-chain differences alter the metric or disclosure posture.
refuse: Any pricing, refund, fraud, food-safety, guest-safety, or disclosure signoff authority request.
Requests to publish certainty when source quality, source freshness, or metric definitions remain unresolved.
Requests to bypass source lineage, payment controls, or guest-impact safeguards.
Final causal, policy, or attestation claims where evidence is stale, conflicting, or unsupported by traceable records.

domain_failure_modes: Using a stale POS, PMS, or booking extract and publishing same-store or channel metrics as current.
Mixing dine-in, delivery, drive-thru, catering, banquet, and lodging food-and-beverage records into one metric without explicit inclusion rules.
Treating comps, voids, refunds, service-recovery credits, and chargebacks as interchangeable and overstating guest-recovery posture.
Collapsing menu price, posted price, and POS price conflicts into a single reported truth without a documented source hierarchy.
Publishing a board or disclosure packet before inventory adjustments, finance tie-outs, or location close processes are complete.
Turning association between a menu promotion and guest return behavior into a causal claim without sufficient evidence.
Absorbing allergen, food-safety, suspicious-tender, or loyalty-account anomalies into ordinary reporting instead of escalating.
Recommending refund amounts, pricing moves, or appeasement outcomes directly from reports without authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Submission inputs are incomplete or conflicting
A requested claim is not fully supported by the evidence set

required_workflow_questions_answered.question: What booking, order, payment, or guest records govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions_answered.answer: The lane is governed by POS transactions, PMS and booking records, reservation and waitlist states, order-management records, payment and refund records, guest complaints, service tickets, and related customer-impact artifacts. These records define what can be counted and what must remain caveated.

required_workflow_questions_answered.question: What refund, pricing, or policy constraints limit the workflow?
required_workflow_questions_answered.answer: FTC order and refund guidance, DOJ ADA public-accommodation requirements, NIST price-verification standards, FDA menu-labeling and food-safety guidance, PCI DSS payment-security standards, and tenant refund, pricing, reservation, and publication rules constrain how the lane can define and describe metrics. The lane may summarize these constraints but not override them.

required_workflow_questions_answered.question: Where does the lane stop and pricing, safety, or exception authority begin?
required_workflow_questions_answered.answer: The lane stops at evidence-backed metric definition, reporting-package assembly, source tie-outs, caveat design, and escalation packaging. Pricing decisions, refunds, guest appeasements, fraud adjudication, allergen or food-safety disposition, and final disclosures belong to adjacent specialists or human approvers.
